titleStmt.title: SII 19.452: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao, Dines Chandra Sircar and G.S. Gai
No. 452. (A.R. No. 318 of 1910.) TIRUMEYJÑĀNAM, KUMBHAKONAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT. ON THE WEST WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, JÑĀNAPARAMĒŚVARA TEMPLE.

summary.p: A few lines at the end of this inscription are lost, the portion being buried under the ground. It registers a sale of 521 kuḻi of land tax-free by the perunguṟi-perumakkaḷ of the mahāsabhā including the baṭṭapperumakkaḷ of Nālūr a brahmadēya in Śēṟṟūr-kūṟṟam for 5 kaḻañju of gold to a merchant of the village named Nāraṉaṉ Tattan, who endowed the same after purchase to the temple of Tirumayānadēva. Among the boundaries of this land are mentioned those belonging to god Tiruppāṟkaḍal-Dēvar Bhiṭāriyār (Bhaṭārī).

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . kopparakecaripanmaṟki yāṇṭu Āvatu ceṟṟūrkkūṟṟattu brahmadeyam ñālūr paṭṭapperumakkaḷuḷḷi
ṭṭa mahāsabhaip peruṅkuṟipperumakkaḷom Ivvūrt tirumayāṉadevacaṇḍeśvararkkuyāṅkaḷ candrādittava
l Iṟaiyiliyāka viṟṟukkuṭutta Eṅkaḷ nilamāvatu kkil viḷākattu niyāyanaṭaivāykāliṉ vaṭa
karai Eṅkaḷ nilamāṉa nilam Ūraṟinta perunāṉkellaiyiluṅ kiḻpāṟkellai kaviṇiyaṉ ṟuveti nākaṉ kira
ṉ makkaḷ nilattukkum niyāyanaṭaiyvāykkālukku meṟkun teṉpāṟkellai niyāyanaṭai vāykkālukku vaṭakkum
melpāṟkellai tirumayāṉadevar nilattukkum tiruppāṟkaṭaṟdevar nilattukkuk kiḻakkum
bhiṭāriyār nilattukkut teṟkku mivvicaitta perunāṉkellaiyilu makappaṭṭa nilaṉ karuttanta pātti 5 100 2 10 1 Ippārtti Aiññūṟṟirupattu Oṉṟum Uṇṇila moḻiviṉṟi varampaṭaṅkal
caṇḍeśvararkku viṟṟukkuṭuttuk koṇṭa poṉ ṟuḷai Aiṅkaḻañcum tippokkuc cempoṉ
kācaṟak koṇṭu candrādittaval Iṟaiyiliyāka viṟṟu vilaiśrāvaṇai ceytukuṭuttom nālūr paṭṭapperumak
kaḷom Ippoṉ kuṭuttu Innilam koṇṭu kuṭutteṉ Ivvūr vyāpāri Aṭiyūruṭaiyāṉ nāraṇaṉ tattaṉāṉa
Digital edition of SII 19.452 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
